items.title: From Good To Bad And Then Back Again
Pigeons & Planes Presents: See You Next Year 3
peace does not reside on my end
Bob Marley Essentials
items.description: Bob Marley had a musical career that spanned three decades and across geographical and cultural boundaries, proving to be instrumental in the popularization of Jamaican music and the Rastafari movement. Marley’s legend has grown to mythical proportions since his death in 1981 as his music, persona and benevolent messages remain beloved worldwide. (Photo: Rovi)

items.title: The Animals Essentials
items.description: One of the most important bands of 'the British Invasion', the Animals were a working-class English group whose dedication to classic folk, blues and R&B was so passionate it became innovative. Case in point: their 1964 hit version of the folksong “House of the Rising Sun,” an exercise in escalating anguish which broke ground for the folk-rock genre and, legend has it, helped inspire Dylan to go electric. Indeed, as interpreters the Animals had few equals among their British peers: Frontman Eric Burdon sang with storied power and grit, and even if the late Hilton Valentine didn’t emerge as a guitar hero per se, the band was a tight, nimble combo given an edge through the organ work of Alan Price. But whereas the Beatles and the Rolling Stones were able to progress from curators of American blues and rock ’n’ roll to master pop songwriters, the relatively short-lived Animals relied mostly on the Brill Building and R&B canon for their hits. Nevertheless, the Animals’ no-nonsense blues-rock archetype informed all the rough-and-ready rock that followed it. (Photo: Rovi)

items.title: Frank Zappa Essentials
items.description: Frank Zappa was one of the most accomplished composers of the rock era. His music shows an affinity for such classical figures as Stockhausen and Varèse, an affection for late 1950s doo wop, and a facility with the guitar-heavy sounds that dominated popular music in the 1970s. But Zappa was also a satirist whose scorn seemed bottomless and whose sense of absurd humor delighted his fans, even when he crossed over the broadest bounds of good taste. Finally, Zappa was perhaps the most prolific record-maker of his time, turning out massive amounts of music on his own Barking Pumpkin label and through distribution deals with Rykodisc and Rhino after long, unhappy associations with industry giants like Warner Brothers and the now-defunct MGM. (Bio: Tivo/Image: UMG)

items.title: Grace Jones Essentials
items.description: The one-of-a-kind Grace Jones is everything: model, singer, actor, performer, diva—a true multi-hyphenate. On the occasion of her new documentary, 'Grace Jones: Bloodlight and Bami,' out April 2018 in North America, TIDAL presents you the ultimate Grace playlist, covering her whole career. Jones started as a disco doyenne, with the legendary Tom Moulton mixing her first full length featuring her minimalist reimagining of Edith Piaf’s “La Vie en Rose.” But after three disco albums, the turn of the decade ushered in her new sound: a new wave post reggae funk androgyny backed by the Compass Point All Stars featuring Sly & Robbie. The trio of records beginning with Warm Leatherette (see her perform the single on Tidal in an exclusive clip) referenced her Jamaican roots but was firmly planted in sleek '80s modernism with striking art. Jones continued releasing records in the '80s, such as 'Slave to the Rhythm,' produced by Trevor Horn, and then collaborated with Nile Rodgers, but after a near 20-year hiatus, she returned with the acclaimed Hurricane in 2008. Ladies and Gentlemen, Miss Grace Jones! (Photo Credit: Getty)

items.title: New Arrivals: Hip-Hop and Latin
items.description: Hip-hop culture is universal and it's evident in the way we consume music today. With the influx of Latin stars on the charts and collaborating with their fellow mainstream hip-hop MCs, it only made sense to combine the worlds. Because at the end of the day, it's all hip-hop, baby. Listen to the best new Hip-Hop & Latin music releases. (Cover: Drake// Photo: Press)

items.title: Dance Craze: 1960s
items.description: Occasionally, a song will become embedded in our culture due to a specific dance that went along to it and soared up the charts and got everyone on the dance floor. The 60s was no exception and "The Twist" by Chubby Checker is the only such song to have gone to #1 twice and also has been named the #1 Song Of All-Time by Billboard Magazine. (Cover: Chubby Checker / Photo: ABKCO, press)

items.title: Drake Essentials
items.description: Consistency is Drake's best trait. Since 2009, the Toronto rapper has amassed an extensive catalog filled with record-breaking highlights and number one hits. Run through the 6 and beyond with Drizzy's best. (Photo: Republic Records, Press)

items.title: Van Halen Essentials
items.description: With their 1978 eponymous debut, Van Halen simultaneously rewrote the rules of rock guitar and hard rock in general. Guitarist Eddie Van Halen redefined what the electric guitar could do, developing a blindingly fast technique with a variety of self-taught two-handed tapping, hammer-ons, pull-offs and effects that mimicked the sounds of machines and animals. It was wildly inventive and over the top, equaled only by vocalist David Lee Roth, who brought the role of a metal singer to near-performance-art standards. Roth wasn’t blessed with great technique, unlike Eddie, but he had a flair for showmanship derived as much from lounge performers as Robert Plant. Together, they made Van Halen into the most popular American rock & roll band of the late ’70s and early ’80s, and in the process set the template for hard rock and heavy metal for the ’80s. (Bio and photo: Rovi)

items.title: Jennifer Lopez Essentials
items.description: Starting out as a Fly Girl (In Living Color) and later an actress, the first Latina to receive over $1 million for a role (Out of Sight), Jennifer Lopez turned her attention to music with 1999’s 'On The 6'. She's since straddled the worlds of pop, hip-hop and Latin, seamlessly. (Photo: Rovi)

items.title: Master P Essentials

promotedArtists.name: Master P, Fiend, Silkk The Shocker, Mia X, Mystikal

items.description: New Orleans native Master P is more than a rapper that transcended southern music. This also-producer and all-around entrepreneur is responsible for spawning the careers of many other great artists, as well as business ventures that have greatly contributed to Hip-Hop culture. Here are some of his biggest hits. (Photo: Rovi)

items.title: Erica Campbell Essentials
items.description: Recording artist, Erica Campbell, began her career in 1998 as a member of the iconic gospel group, Mary Mary. Since then, she has created her own lane as an author, influencer and radio host of "Get Up! Mornings With Erica Campbell." These are her essentials. (Photo: Rovi)

items.title: Mary Mary Essentials
items.description: Listen to the most essential tracks from Mary Mary. (Photo: Rovi)
